note: Editor Advanced Link is a CKEditor 5 plugin configured per text format with no admin route of its own, so all cases are `recipe` (response-graded), not `execution` (live-state).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I enabled the Editor Advanced Link module. What is the machine name / plugin id of the CKEditor 5 plugin it provides (the one that adds the advanced link attributes), and what is its human-readable label in the CKEditor 5 plugin settings?
evals.expected_output: The CKEditor 5 plugin id is editor_advanced_link_link, labelled "Advanced links" in the CKEditor 5 plugin settings vertical tabs.

evals.prompt: I installed Editor Advanced Link but can't find an admin page for it. Where do I configure it, and what makes the Advanced links plugin actually appear in a text format's CKEditor 5 settings?
evals.expected_output: There is no dedicated admin route. Configure it per text format at Admin > Configuration > Content authoring > Text formats and editors (/admin/config/content/formats). The Advanced links plugin activates automatically when the core Link (ckeditor5_link) plugin is enabled on that CKEditor 5 format.

evals.prompt: Which extra <a> attributes can Editor Advanced Link expose in the CKEditor link dialog? List them.
evals.expected_output: The supported attributes (SUPPORTED_ATTRIBUTES) are: aria-label, title, class, id, target (Open in new window, target="_blank"), and rel.

evals.prompt: My text format uses "Limit allowed HTML tags and correct faulty HTML". Editors can't see the Title field on links from Editor Advanced Link even though I enabled the Title attribute in the Advanced links plugin. What must I change in the allowed HTML tags, and generally how do the two settings interact?
evals.expected_output: An attribute is only usable when it is allowed on the <a> tag in the filter's allowed HTML. Enabling the attribute in the plugin contributes the matching fragment (e.g. <a title>) via getElementsSubset(); if you tighten the filter you must ensure <a title> (or <a class>, <a id>, <a rel>, <a aria-label>, <a target="_blank">) is present in the allowed tags.

evals.prompt: In Editor Advanced Link, what happens when I check "Open in new window" in the Advanced links plugin settings — what does it write to the link and how?
evals.expected_output: Checking "Open in new window" registers a manual CKEditor link decorator (via getDynamicPluginConfig) that writes target="_blank" on the link, and contributes the <a target="_blank"> allowed-HTML fragment.

evals.prompt: Where and under what key are the Editor Advanced Link attribute choices stored in a text format's exported configuration, and what is the config schema key?
evals.expected_output: The checked attributes are stored as enabled_attributes (a sequence of attribute strings) under the editor's settings.plugins.editor_advanced_link_link config. The schema key is ckeditor5.plugin.editor_advanced_link_link. Default is enabled_attributes: [] (nothing exposed until opted in).

evals.prompt: I use the classic editor link dialog and Linkit rather than the CKEditor 5 plugin path. How does Editor Advanced Link add its advanced fields (title, class, id, target, rel, aria-label) to those dialogs?
evals.expected_output: editor_advanced_link.module implements hook_form_editor_link_dialog_alter() (editor_advanced_link_form_editor_link_dialog_alter) to add the fields, and editor_advanced_link_form_linkit_editor_dialog_form_alter() delegates to the same alter so Linkit's dialog gets them too. Field #access is computed from the format's HTML restrictions.

evals.prompt: We are upgrading a site from CKEditor 4 to CKEditor 5. Does Editor Advanced Link's old configuration carry over, and what handles that mapping?
evals.expected_output: Yes. src/Plugin/CKEditor4To5Upgrade/AdvancedLink.php (plugin id editor_advanced_link) maps the old CKEditor 4 configuration to the CKEditor 5 editor_advanced_link_link plugin during the core upgrade path, preserving advanced-link behavior.
